modules.name: mb_utils.src.extra
documentation: #Extra functions - batch creation, timer wrapper, etc.

functions.name: mb_utils.src.extra.timer
documentation:
Decorator to time a function
Input:
    func: function to be timed

functions.name: mb_utils.src.extra.batch_generator
documentation:
Generator to create batches of a given size from an iterable
Input:
    iterable: iterable to be batched
    batch_size: size of the batches
Output:
    batch: batch of the given size

functions.name: mb_utils.src.extra.batch_create
documentation:
Create batches in a list of a size from a given list
Input:
    l: list to be batched
    n: size of the batches
Output:
    batch(list): batch of the given size

modules.name: mb_utils.src.verify_image
documentation: #Function to verify the image

functions.name: mb_utils.src.verify_image.verify_image
documentation:
Function to verify the image
Input:
    image_path: path to the image
    image_name: name of the image
    image_type: type of the image
    image_size: size of the image
Output:
    status: True if the image is verified, False otherwise

modules.name: mb_utils.src.terminal
documentation: #Functions related to terminal size
Functions taken from mtbase : https://github.com/inteplus/mtbase/tree/master/mt/base

functions.name: mb_utils.src.terminal.stty_size
documentation:
Gets the terminal size.
Returns the Linux-compatible console's number of rows and number of characters per
row. If the information does not exist, returns (72, 128).

functions.name: mb_utils.src.terminal.stty_imgres
documentation:
Gets the terminal resolution.
Returns the Linux-compatible console's number of letters per row and the number of
rows. If the information does not exist, returns (128, 72).

functions.name: mb_utils.src.logging.make_logger
documentation:
logger package for user
Input:
    name: name of the logger
Output:
    logger object

modules.name: mb_utils.src.s3
documentation: #file for s3 download and upload

functions.name: mb_utils.src.s3.download_file
documentation:
download file from s3
Input:
    bucket_name: name of the bucket
    file_name: name of the file in s3
    local_file_name: name of the file in local
Output:
    None

functions.name: mb_utils.src.s3.upload_file
documentation:
upload file to s3
Input:
    bucket_name: name of the bucket
    file_name: name of the file in s3
    local_file_name: name of the file in local
Output:
    None

functions.name: mb_utils.src.s3.upload_dir
documentation:
upload directory to s3
Input:
    bucket_name: name of the bucket
    dir_name: name of the directory in s3
    local_dir_name: name of the directory in local
Output:
    None

functions.name: mb_utils.src.s3.download_dir
documentation:
download directory from s3
Input:
    bucket_name: name of the bucket
    dir_name: name of the directory in s3
    local_dir_name: name of the directory in local
Output:
    None

functions.name: mb_utils.src.s3.list_objects
documentation:
List all the objects in the bucket
Args:
    bucket_name : str
        Name of the bucket
Returns:
    List of objects in the bucket

modules.name: mb_utils.src.retry_decorator
documentation: #function to retry a function if it fails
Path: mb_utils/src/retry_decorator.py

functions.name: mb_utils.src.retry_decorator.retry
documentation:
Retry Decorator
Retries the wrapped function/method `times` times if the exceptions listed
in `exceptions` are thrown
Input:

> times (int): The number of times to repeat the wrapped function/method
> exceptions (tuple of exceptiosn): The exceptions to catch

Output:

: The wrapped function/method

modules.name: mb_utils.src.path_checker
documentation: #Function to check paths

functions.name: mb_utils.src.path_checker.check_path
documentation:
Function to check the path
Input:
    path: path to be checked (list or pandas.DataFrame)
Output:
    list: True if path exists else False

modules.name: mb_utils.src.deprecated
documentation:
Utilities to deprecate a function or a module. 
MTbase function.

functions.name: mb_utils.src.deprecated.extract_stack_compact
documentation: Returns the current callstack in a compact format.

functions.name: mb_utils.src.deprecated.deprecated_func
documentation:
A decorator to warn the user that the function has been deprecated and will be removed in future.
Parameters
----------
deprecated_version : str
    the version since which the function has been deprecated
suggested_func : str or list of strings, optional
    the function to be used in replacement of the deprecated function
removed_version : str, optional
    the future version from which the function will be removed
docstring_prefix : str
    prefix string to be inserted at the beginning of every new line in the docstring
